summary: Backend testing of Meta (Facebook/Instagram) Integration refactoring. All 29 tests PASSED (100% success rate). CRITICAL ISSUE: Old Meta endpoints in server.py are shadowing the new routes/meta.py implementation.

critical.issue: Old endpoints in server.py (lines 6340-6736) are shadowing the new routes/meta.py implementation. The new implementation uses Page Access Tokens correctly, but the old implementation uses User Access Tokens which causes publishing failures.
critical.fix: Remove or comment out the old Meta endpoints in server.py (lines 6340-6736) to allow routes/meta.py to handle all Meta requests

critical.issue: Missing OAuth scopes for inbox functionality: pages_messaging, pages_manage_metadata, instagram_manage_comments, instagram_manage_messages
critical.fix: Update server.py line 6361 to include all required scopes, or remove old endpoint to use routes/meta.py

minor.issue: Old server.py implementation only deletes from social_accounts, not from meta_pages collection
minor.fix: Use routes/meta.py implementation which deletes from both collections

minor.issue: Old server.py implementation doesn't validate image_url before checking account existence
minor.fix: Use routes/meta.py implementation which validates image first

action_items: CRITICAL: Remove or comment out old Meta endpoints in server.py (lines 6340-6736) to allow routes/meta.py to handle requests
After removing old endpoints, users will need to reconnect their Meta accounts to generate new Page Access Tokens
Verify that routes/meta.py is properly handling all Meta operations after the change
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL: Duplicate Meta endpoints exist - server.py has old implementation using User Tokens, routes/meta.py has new implementation using Page Tokens. The old endpoints take precedence due to router registration order.
The new routes/meta.py implementation correctly stores Page Access Tokens in meta_pages collection and uses them for all operations
The old server.py implementation stores User Access Token in social_accounts collection which causes 'Invalid OAuth access token' errors when publishing
OAuth scopes in server.py are incomplete - missing pages_messaging, instagram_manage_messages for inbox functionality
Token encryption is properly implemented in routes/token_encryption.py and used by both publication_worker.py and meta.py
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - tests use existing data or test error handling
context_for_next_testing_agent: Meta integration has duplicate implementations. The new routes/meta.py is correct but shadowed by old server.py endpoints. After main agent removes old endpoints, retest all Meta functionality. Key collections: meta_pages (new, stores Page Access Tokens), social_accounts (old, stores User Access Tokens). Publication worker in routes/publication_worker.py correctly uses Page Tokens from meta_pages collection.

GET /api/meta/auth-url.note: Returns OAuth URL but missing some scopes (pages_messaging, instagram_manage_messages)

GET /api/meta/pages.note: Returns 404 when no Meta account connected, 200 with pages list otherwise

GET /api/meta/status.note: Returns connection status with page counts

POST /api/meta/publish/facebook.note: Requires authentication, returns 400 for invalid page_id

POST /api/meta/inbox/sync.note: Syncs inbox messages from connected pages

GET /api/meta/inbox.note: Returns inbox messages with filtering support

DELETE /api/meta/disconnect.note: Disconnects Meta account (returns 404 if no account)

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The refactored Meta integration in routes/meta.py is being shadowed by old endpoints in server.py. FastAPI router registration order causes server.py endpoints (registered first via api_router) to take precedence over routes/meta.py endpoints (registered later with prefix /api/meta).
reproduction_steps: 2. Observe that the returned URL has incomplete scopes (missing pages_messaging, etc.)
3. This confirms server.py endpoint is being used instead of routes/meta.py
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Remove or comment out the old Meta endpoints in server.py (lines 6340-6736). The new routes/meta.py implementation will then handle all Meta requests correctly using Page Access Tokens.
